DISCOVERY 610, APPEARANCES: ISSA J. MICHAEL FOR DEFENDANT AND MOVING PARTY: ISLAMIC SOCIETY OF SAN FRANCISCO KENNETH N. FRUCHT, FOR PLAINTIFF AND OPPOSING PARTY: SAFWAR MORSY ARGUED AND TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED WITH MODIFICATIONS: DEFENDANT'S MOTION FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS IS GRANTED. FAILURE TO PROVIDE RESPONSES WAS A MISUSE OF THE DISCOVERY PROCESS. MONETARY SANCTIONS IN THE AMOUNT OF $936.30 ARE TO BE PAID ON OR BEFORE FEBRUARY 16, 2005. PLAINTIFF IS ORDERED TO PROVIDE COMPLETE VERIFIED RESPONSES WITHOUT OBJECTION PURSUANT TO THE NOVEMBER 24, 2004 ORDER BY FEBRUARY 16, 2005. THE COURT ORDERS THAT THE PARTIES MEET AND CONFER TO SCHEDULE THE DEPOSITION OF PLAINTIFF. MODIFICATION: MOTION FOR TERMINATING SANCTIONS IS DENIED. NO FURTHER HEARING REQUIRED. SANCTIONS OF $936.30 DUE 2/16/05. COMM: BRUCE E. CHAN

DISCOVERY 610, DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO ENFORCE COMPLIANCE WITH THE PRIOR ORDER TO PRODUCE THE PERSON MOST KNOWLEDGEABLE AND DOCUMENTS IS GRANTED. AL-SABEEL IS ORDERED TO PRODUCE THE PERSON MOST KNOWLEDGEABLE AND THE ORIGINALS OF ANY AND ALL CHECKS PROVIDED TO PLAINTIFF WITHIN 15 DAYS OF NOTICE OF THIS ORDER. THE COURT FINDS THAT THE DEPONENT FAILED TO COMPLY WITH THE PREVIOUS ORDER AND ASSERTED UNMERITORIOUS OBJECTIONS. MONETARY SANCTIONS AGAINST AL-SABEEL IN THE AMOUNT OF $786.30 ARE TO BE PAID WITHIN 30 DAYS OF NOTICE OF THIS ORDER. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN (610)

Notice Of Motion To Enforce Al Sabeel To Comply With Court Order To Produce Pmk And Documents, And Sanctions
Off Calendar Dec-21-2005 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Issa J. Michael, Esq. for defendant/moving party, Islamic Society of San Francisco
Kenneth N. Frucht, Esq. for plaintiff/opposing party, Safwat Morsy

Matter argued. After the argument, the Court adopted the tentative ruling. Defendant's motion to enforce compliance with the prior order to produce the person most knowledgeable and documents is granted. Al-Sabeel is ordered to produce the person most knowledgeable and the originals of any and all checks provided to plaintiff within 15 days of notice of this order. The Court finds that the deponent failed to comply with the previous order and asserted unmeritorious objections. Monetary sanctions against Al-Sabeel in the amount of $786.30 are to be paid within 30 days of notice of this order. (610/BEC)

Motion For Protective Order And For Sanctions In Connection With Depos Of Plaintiff
Off Calendar Jun-15-2005 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Issa Michael for defendant and moving party: Islamic Society of San Francisco
Kenneth N. Frucht for plaintiff and opposing party: Safwat Morsy
HEARING HELD and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant's Motion For Protective Order and For Sanctions In Connection With Deposition Of Plaintiff is granted as it seeks an order barring the presence of Mohammed Allababidi in the deposition. While Mr. Allababidi may be present at the site of the deposition, his status as a witness is good cause to bar him from being present in the room while deposition testimony is being given. Defendant's request for sanctions is denied. ORDER SIGNED in open court. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT
